pages.raw_text:
Electrical contractors declared an
open shop today. They say they
will sign no agreement making any
concessions to the unlCn. Five dol
lars per day is being paid for non
union help, but it is scarce at that,
only a few men having so far been
procured. The contractors say they
will give employment to the union
electrical workers upon the same
basis if they desire to work with
nonunion employees.


Every available union electrical
worker has been picked up since
yesterday morning and has been
given work either with the Journey
men. who have begun contracting
for themselves since the beginning
of the deadlock. or with the 3 shops
which have signed the Spokane
Building Trades alliance agree
ment.


Efforts are being made today to
secure helpers. Many of the union
electrical workers have left the
city since the beginning of the
trouble and have found employ
ment in Seattle and other coast
cities. Secretary Allen, of the cleo
trical workers, who has been in
Walla Walla organizing an cleo
trical workers union, will return to
Spokase tomorrow.
